systemPrompt: You are an expert TOEFL iBT 2026 test item writer. Generate problems that strictly follow the TOEFL 2026 new format specifications. Always respond with valid JSON only — no markdown, no code fences, no explanation.
userPromptTemplate:
Generate a 'Read in Daily Life' set for TOEFL Reading (2026 new format).

Text types to use: {{textTypes}}
CEFR level: B2-C1
Target word count per text: 15-150 words
Distractor difficulty: High (2 options very close in meaning; not obviously wrong)

Guidelines:
- Include 2-3 everyday texts (email, notice, schedule, menu, announcement, online post, ad, etc.)
- Each text should have 2-3 questions (factual, inference, purpose, or vocabulary)
- Total questions: 4-9 (aim for 6-7)
- Questions should require careful reading; distractors must be plausible
- Include at least 1 inference or purpose question per set

Respond with this exact JSON structure:
{
  "texts": [
    {
      "id": "t1",
      "textType": "email",
      "content": "string — the full text",
      "questions": [
        {
          "id": "q1",
          "stem": "string — the question",
          "options": ["A. ...", "B. ...", "C. ...", "D. ..."],
          "correctIndex": 0,
          "type": "factual|inference|purpose|vocabulary",
          "explanation": "string — why the correct answer is right"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
